svg.title: Decision tree: agents vs workflow patterns
svg.desc: A flowchart helping developers decide between single LLM calls, workflow patterns (prompt chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer), and autonomous agents.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What should I consider before building an agentic system?')
sendPrompt('When is a single LLM call enough?')
g.text: Can a single prompt solve it?

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I optimize a single LLM call with retrieval?')
sendPrompt('What is the difference between a fixed workflow and a dynamic agent?')
g.text: even if the LLM decides details at runtime?

g.onclick: sendPrompt('When should I use an autonomous agent instead of a workflow?')
sendPrompt('How do I pick the right workflow pattern?')
g.text: What is the structure of the task?

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show me an example of prompt chaining in Python')
sendPrompt('Show me an example of a routing workflow in Python')
sendPrompt('Show me an example of parallelization workflow in Python')
sendPrompt('When should I use orchestrator-workers vs evaluator-optimizer?')
g.text: Do subtasks emerge at runtime, or does

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show me an example of the orchestrator-workers pattern in Python')
sendPrompt('Show me an example of the evaluator-optimizer pattern in Python')
sendPrompt('How do I know when I need a true autonomous agent?')
g.text: Steps truly unpredictable, trust is high,

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What are the three core principles for building effective agents?')
g.text: LLM + tools in a loop, self-directed, sandboxed
extensive testing · stopping conditions · human checkpoints
